headlines.title: 'People flew from their seats': Passengers describe how collision unfolded
headlines.description: The East of England Ambulance Service says nearly 90 people were injured, over 30 of them seriously.

headlines.title: PM under pressure from Labour MPs and ministers to set timetable for exit
headlines.description: The BBC has been told that the transport secretary is among those advising Starmer to set out a timetable.

headlines.title: Several reported killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon despite ceasefire
headlines.description: The IDF said it struck "Hezbollah terrorist targets" after the group fired over 50 projectiles at Israeli forces in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: Cooler Saturday gives brief relief before heat returns
headlines.description: After a slightly cooler Saturday, temperatures will rise again, possibly touching 35C by Tuesday with a Met Office amber extreme heat warning set to come into force.

headlines.title: Harry and Meghan offered royal accommodation during UK visit
headlines.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex are set to visit the UK with their children next month, for the first time since 2022.

headlines.title: What we know so far about train collision and emergency response
headlines.description: Two passenger trains collided, leaving a driver dead and dozens of passengers and crew hurt.

headlines.title: Video shows scene of collision as passenger describes aftermath
headlines.description: Passenger Pete Knapp said the crash "felt like [he'd] been in a bomb explosion".

headlines.title: Flawed but relentless Scotland show themselves as men of substance
headlines.description: Morocco threatened to blow Scotland away early on, but Steve Clarke's squad showed their resilience despite defeat, writes Tom English.

headlines.title: World Cup still waits for real Brazil to show up
headlines.description: While rivals Argentina and France have impressed in the United States, Brazil have struggled to hit top form - despite a 3-0 win over minnows Haiti.

headlines.title: Paraguay's Almiron first player sent off for covering mouth
headlines.description: Paraguay's Miguel Almiron is the first player to be shown a red card for covering his mouth while speaking to an opponent - doing so during the World Cup match against Turkey.

headlines.title: Nervy, optimist, die-hard: What sort of fan are you?
headlines.description: Take our World Cup quiz and discover your fan personality type.

headlines.title: Popping a cocktail of supplements every day might be doing you more harm than good
headlines.description: While many of us take supplements regularly, there are some risks people may not be aware of.

headlines.title: Love Pride but hate big parades? These places are doing it differently
headlines.description: From Glasgow to Salford, smaller, unique events are celebrating the LGBTQ+ community.

headlines.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
headlines.description: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

headlines.title: A fox, a gannet and an intergalactic space warrior - why UK election candidates dress up
headlines.description: Candidates like Count Binface are motivated to run for different reasons, but their role in politics is a longstanding tradition.

headlines.title: How to stay safe swimming outdoors - and still have fun
headlines.description: Pick designated swimming spots, learn about riptides and don't use inflatables at the beach, experts say.

headlines.title: Child attacked by crocodile critical two days on: Here's what we know so far
headlines.description: A 30-year-old man is bailed after a child ended up in a crocodile enclosure at a farm zoo.

headlines.title: Tourist dies in Dominican Republic luxury resort fire
headlines.description: Nearly 1,700 people were evacuated from the huge blaze at Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach in Bayahibe, a popular resort town on the Caribbean coast.

headlines.title: New video game console aims to get kids moving
headlines.description: The cube-shaped console will cost £269 (€319) when it is released on 22 June in the UK and Ireland.

headlines.title: Michael Fassbender says it is becoming harder to know what to trust online
headlines.description: Stars of spy thriller show The Agency discuss espionage, AI and the personal cost of living a double life.

headlines.title: 'My dog Gertie saved my life by helping to find my breast cancer'
headlines.description: Lesley Goodburn is hoping her story will encourage others to check for signs of the cancer.

headlines.title: Paddington musical set for Broadway debut
headlines.description: The production will launch in the US at New York's Al Hirschfeld Theatre in March 2027.

headlines.title: Sherpa travels thousands of miles to thank children for help after earthquake
headlines.description: A Nepalese Sherpa travels thousands of miles to thank children for their fundraising efforts.

headlines.title: Snake takes surprise trip to seaside in car engine
headlines.description: The corn snake is thought to have travelled around 17 miles from its owner's house in Bridlington.

headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Burnham Wins in Makerfield - So What Happens Now?
headlines.description: Burnham wins Makerfield by-election, paving way for leadership challenge against Starmer.

headlines.title: Jill Biden reveals how she really felt about Joe Biden’s health whilst he was president
headlines.description: And Trump wants to give America’s top legal job to his former personal lawyer
